author: xinlingfeiwu
title: Systematic Over-Injection Anti-Pattern: Constants Passed as Parameters Throughout the Review Pipeline
notes: All three layers of over-injection are confirmed. build_review_context_inner takes 10+ keyword params (including 4 regex constants) from a single call site that always passes the same values. prepare_holistic_review_payload has 14 _fn params with one production caller and tests that monkeypatch at the module boundary instead. CodexBatchRunnerDeps/FollowupScanDeps prove the codebase has a better pattern. Caveats: DI for functions (not regex constants) is a legitimate Python pattern even with one call site; the regex constants are the clearest anti-pattern case; this affects maintainability but causes no bugs.
